description_long: This object is a delicate flowering plant with three distinct blossoms, each featuring five elongated, pointed petals in a deep reddish hue. The petals are arranged in a somewhat radial pattern, with some overlapping and slight asymmetry, giving the flowers a natural, organic appearance. The stems are thin, green, and gently curved, branching out from a central point and supporting the blossoms at varying heights and angles. There are no visible leaves attached to the stems, emphasizing the flowers as the main visual focus. The overall structure is airy and lightweight, with the blossoms spaced apart and connected by slender stems. The coloration is primarily a rich red for the petals and a pale green for the stems, with subtle shading to suggest depth and texture.
description: A slender, branching plant with three red, five-petaled flowers on thin green stems and no visible leaves. The blossoms are spaced apart, each with pointed petals arranged in a loose radial pattern.
description_view_0: Three red flowers with pointed petals are visible, two on the left and one on the right, all connected by thin green stems.
description_view_1: The flowers are seen more from the side, with the rightmost blossom appearing elongated and the stems arching outward.
description_view_2: The central flower is most prominent, with the other two blossoms extending to the left and right, showing the spread of the stems.
description_view_3: The lower flower is fully visible with all five petals, while the other two blossoms are seen from the side, highlighting the curvature of the stems.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a loose radial pattern, with five petals per flower, some overlapping and slightly asymmetrical, lacking perfect radial symmetry.
